objective: Create internal/provider/executor.go (added to the EXISTING `package provider`) defining an `Executor` struct (holds the repo-root `Dir string`) with method `Run(ctx context.Context, m Manifest, model, provider, sys, payload string) (string, error)` that: (1) resolves model/provider defaults from the manifest (`if model=="" { model=m.DefaultModel }`, `if provider=="" { provider=m.DefaultProvider }`) THEN calls `m.Render(model, provider, sys, payload)` (the PURE renderer from P1.M2.T1.S1); (2) builds `exec.Command(m.Command, rendered.Args...)` with NO `sh -c` (§19), `cmd.Dir=e.Dir` (repo root), `cmd.Stdin=bytes.NewReader([]byte(rendered.StdinPayload))` when `rendered.DeliverViaStdin` else nil (os/exec gives the child /dev/null automatically), `cmd.Env=append(os.Environ(), rendered.Env...)`, and `cmd.SysProcAttr=&syscall.SysProcAttr{Setpgid:true}` (§18.4) so the agent + its children form one killable process group; (3) captures stdout into a bytes.Buffer and stderr into a second buffer; (4) starts the process, runs `cmd.Wait()` in a goroutine, and selects on the `done` channel vs `ctx.Done()`; on `ctx.Done()` it sends SIGTERM to the whole group (`syscall.Kill(-pgid, syscall.SIGTERM)`), waits up to ~2s for exit, then SIGKILLs the group, draining the goroutine, and returns a typed `*TimeoutError` (when `ctx.Err()==context.DeadlineExceeded`) or `ctx.Err()` (`context.Canceled`); on a clean child exit it returns the captured stdout; on a non-zero exit it returns a typed `*AgentError` carrying `m.Name`, `m.Command`, the exit code, and a short (~500 char) stderr excerpt. The `context.Context` parameter IS the seam the signal handler (M6.T2.S1) observes: CommitStaged owns the cancellable context, passes `ctx` to Run and the cancel func to the signal handler, so a SIGINT/SIGTERM cancels ctx → Run kills the group. Deliver executor_test.go (package provider, stdlib `testing`, real host binaries `/bin/echo`, `/bin/cat`, `/bin/sh`, `/bin/sleep` as the fake agents — NO real LLM, NO stub-binary generator): assert stdin is fed exactly (cat echoes verbatim), stdout is captured (echo), a non-zero-exit fake returns `*AgentError` with the stderr excerpt, a hanging stub under a `context.WithTimeout` is process-group-killed and returns `*TimeoutError` (~80ms, proving SIGTERM killed it fast), and an explicitly-cancelled ctx returns `context.Canceled`; one test uses `/bin/sh -c 'trap "" TERM; sleep 30'` under a timeout to prove BOTH group targeting (-PGID) AND the 2s-grace SIGKILL escalation. DOCS impact: Mode A — godoc on Executor/Run/TimeoutError/AgentError citing PRD §18.4, FR24, FR25, §19.
context:
# PRP — P1.M2.T4.S1: internal/provider/executor.go — Executor.Run (render→exec→timeout→process-group-kill)

## Goal

**Feature Goal**: Implement the robust subprocess executor that turns a resolved provider intent into a real, timeout-enforced, process-group-isolated agent run: it renders the manifest (P1.M2.T1.S1), execs the agent command directly with a `[]string` arg slice (NEVER `sh -c`, §19), feeds the payload via stdin, captures stdout, and on timeout/cancel kills the agent AND all its children as a single process group (§18.4: `SysProcAttr.Setpgid=true` + `kill(-pgid)` with a ~2s grace SIGKILL), returning typed errors the generate orchestrator (M6.T1) and rescue path (M6.T1.S3) route on.

**Deliverable**: Two new files in the EXISTING `internal/provider` package (created by P1.M2.T1.S1; this task only ADDS files, plain `package provider` line — the `// Package provider` doc stays in manifest.go):
- `internal/provider/executor.go` — `Executor` struct (`Dir string`), `NewExecutor(dir string) *Executor` constructor, `func (e *Executor) Run(ctx context.Context, m Manifest, model, provider, sys, payload string) (string, error)`, typed errors `TimeoutError` + `AgentError`.
- `internal/provider/executor_test.go` — `package provider`, stdlib `testing` only; uses real host binaries as fake agents.

**Success Definition**: `go test ./internal/provider/` and `go test ./...` pass; `go build ./internal/provider/`, `go vet ./internal/provider/` clean; `gofmt -l internal/provider/` empty. Tests prove: stdin fed byte-exactly, stdout captured, non-zero exit → `*AgentError` w/ stderr excerpt, timeout → process-group SIGTERM (fast) + `*TimeoutError`, explicit cancel → `context.Canceled`, SIGTERM-ignoring child → 2s-grace SIGKILL. stdlib-only imports (`context`, `os/exec`, `syscall`, `bytes`, `time`, `fmt`, `strings`, `errors`); no go.mod/go.sum change; manifest.go/parse.go/registry.go/builtin.go/main.go/Makefile/internal/ui untouched; no import cycle.

## Why

- **The runtime half of the provider subsystem.** P1.M2.T1.S1 delivered the PURE `Manifest.Render` (turns an intent into a `[]string` Args + stdin payload + env additions, no I/O). This task is the OTHER half — the only place that actually spawns the agent. Every agent invocation in the product flows through `Executor.Run`: the generate inner loop (M6.T1.S1), the stub-provider integration harness (M6.T3.S1), and the real-agent opt-in suite (M8.T3) all call `Run`. Getting the timeout + process-group kill correct here is what makes FR25 (timeout→kill→rescue) and §18.4 (kill the whole tree on signal) hold.
- **Process-group kill is the whole point of `Setpgid`.** Agents spawn children (sub-shells, MCP servers, language servers). Killing only the leader PID leaks orphans that keep the terminal hostage and skew timing. `SysProcAttr{Setpgid:true}` makes the agent a group leader (PGID==PID) so `syscall.Kill(-pgid, sig)` fells the entire tree at once. This is the genuinely-new engineering surface (reference_impl.md §7 lists it as NOT in the proven `commit-pi`, which just `trap`'d).
- **`context.Context` is the cancellation seam the signal handler needs.** The work item demands the signal handler (M6.T2.S1) be able to "share/observe this context". The M6.T2.S1 contract confirms the mechanism: CommitStaged owns a cancellable context, threads `ctx` into `Run` and the cancel func into the signal handler; a SIGINT/SIGTERM calls cancel → `ctx.Done()` fires in Run → Run kills the group. So `Run(ctx context.Context, ...)` accepting ctx and reacting to `ctx.Done()` IS that seam — no extra field/accessor is needed.
- **Security is structural (§19).** By exec'ing `m.Command` directly with a `[]string` (never `sh -c`), and delivering the diff via stdin (never interpolated into an arg), Run closes the entire shell-injection class. Render already produced a clean `[]string`; Run must not re-stringify it.

## What (technical requirements)

1. NEW file `internal/provider/executor.go`, plain `package provider` line (manifest.go OWNS the `// Package provider` doc — do NOT repeat it; sibling files parse.go/registry.go/builtin.go already use a plain `package provider` line — mirror them).
2. **`Executor` struct** — minimal, holds only what Run needs now: `Dir string` (the repo root passed to `cmd.Dir`; per the work item "cmd.Dir = repo root"). Do NOT add speculative fields (no logger/writer — land those when M6 wires a verbose UI handle). Provide `func NewExecutor(dir string) *Executor { return &Executor{Dir: dir} }` for ergonomics.
3. **Typed errors** (exported, so generate/rescue can `errors.As` them):
   - `type TimeoutError struct { Deadline time.Time }` — returned when the run's context deadline elapsed AND the group was killed. `Error()` returns e.g. `"provider: generation timed out"`; `Unwrap() error { return context.DeadlineExceeded }` so `errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded)` is true (lets callers treat it uniformly).
   - `type AgentError struct { Name string; Command string; Code int; Stderr string }` — returned on non-zero child exit. `Name`=m.Name, `Command`=m.Command, `Code`=exit code, `Stderr`=short excerpt (last ~500 chars, trimmed). `Error()` formats a clear one-liner including the command + code + excerpt.
4. **`Run(ctx context.Context, m Manifest, model, provider, sys, payload string) (string, error)`** implementing the full flow (see Implementation Blueprint for the verified pseudocode):
   - **Resolve defaults** (per the P1.M2.T1.S1 integration seam — Render is pure and does NOT resolve defaults): `if model == "" { model = m.DefaultModel }`; `if provider == "" { provider = m.DefaultProvider }`.
   - **Render**: `r, err := m.Render(model, provider, sys, payload)`; on error return `"", err` (Render returns an error only for unknown prompt_delivery).
   - **Build the command**: `cmd := exec.Command(m.Command, r.Args...)` (Args EXCLUDE the command token — Render guarantees this). `cmd.Dir = e.Dir`. `if r.DeliverViaStdin { cmd.Stdin = bytes.NewReader([]byte(r.StdinPayload)) }` (else leave nil ⇒ os/exec connects the child's stdin to /dev/null automatically). `cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), r.Env...)` (`r.Env` are the manifest's sorted `"K=V"` additions). `cmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}`. `var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer; cmd.Stdout = &stdout; cmd.Stderr = &stderr`.
   - **Start + goroutine + select**: `if err := cmd.Start(); err != nil { return "", fmt.Errorf(...) }`. Capture `pgid := cmd.Process.Pid` (with Setpgid, the child's PID equals its PGID). Run `done := make(chan error, 1); go func() { done <- cmd.Wait() }()`. Then `select`:
     - `case err := <-done:` — process exited on its own. If `err == nil` return `stdout.String(), nil`. Else (non-zero exit / unexpected signal) return `"", &AgentError{Name: m.Name, Command: m.Command, Code: exitCodeOf(err), Stderr: excerpt(stderr.String())}`.
     - `case <-ctx.Done():` — timeout or signal-driven cancel. `cause := ctx.Err()`. Kill the group: `syscall.Kill(-pgid, syscall.SIGTERM)`, then `select { case <-done: case <-time.After(2*time.Second): syscall.Kill(-pgid, syscall.SIGKILL); <-done }` (drain the goroutine so it does not leak). If `cause == context.DeadlineExceeded` return `"", &TimeoutError{Deadline: <ctx deadline or time.Now()>}`; else return `"", cause` (a `*context.canceled` → `context.Canceled`).
   - **Timeout ownership**: Run takes NO timeout parameter (the work-item signature has none). FR25's configurable timeout (cfg.Timeout, default 120s) is applied by the CALLER (CommitStaged does `context.WithTimeout(parent, cfg.Timeout)`). Run merely distinguishes `DeadlineExceeded` (→TimeoutError) from `Canceled` (→cancel error).
5. **Helpers**: `exitCodeOf(err error) int` — unwrap `*exec.ExitError` and return `ExitCode()`; return -1 if not an exit error (signaled-unexpected). `excerpt(s string) string` — `strings.TrimSpace` the last ~500 chars of the stderr buffer (short per the work item "short stderr excerpt").
6. **Signal-handler seam**: do NOT add a stored context, a public Cancel method, or an observe channel. The `ctx` PARAMETER is the seam — document in Run's godoc that the caller (generate/CommitStaged) owns the cancellable context and threads the cancel func to the signal handler (M6.T2.S1), whose cancellation triggers the group kill via this select.
7. NEW file `internal/provider/executor_test.go`, `package provider`, stdlib `testing` ONLY. Fake agents are REAL host binaries (NO stub-binary generator, NO real LLM):
   - `TestRun_StdinFedExactly`: Manifest{Command:"/bin/cat", PromptDelivery:"stdin"}; payload="FED-EXACT"; assert `out=="FED-EXACT"`, `err==nil`.
   - `TestRun_StdoutCaptured`: Manifest{Command:"/bin/echo", Subcommand:["hi"]} (or Command "/bin/echo" + a rendered positional) ⇒ `out=="hi\n"`, `err==nil`.
   - `TestRun_AgentErrorNonZeroExit`: Manifest{Command:"/bin/sh", Subcommand:["-c","echo oops >&2; exit 7"]}; assert `err` is `*AgentError` via `errors.As`, `.Code==7`, `.Stderr` contains "oops", `out==""`.
   - `TestRun_TimeoutKillsGroup`: Manifest{Command:"/bin/sleep", Subcommand:["30"]}; `ctx,cancel:=context.WithTimeout(ctx,80*time.Millisecond)`; assert `err` is `*TimeoutError`, `errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded)` true, elapsed < ~1s (proves SIGTERM killed it fast — no 2s grace needed).
   - `TestRun_CancelReturnsCanceled`: same sleep stub; `ctx,cancel:=context.WithCancel(ctx)`; cancel after 40ms in a goroutine; assert `errors.Is(err, context.Canceled)`.
   - `TestRun_GraceSIGKILLOnIgnoredTERM`: Manifest{Command:"/bin/sh", Subcommand:["-c","trap '' TERM; sleep 30"]} (child ignores SIGTERM); `context.WithTimeout(ctx, 80ms)`; assert `*TimeoutError` returned AND elapsed ≥ ~2s, < ~3s (proves the 2s-grace SIGKILL escalation fired — the child would NOT die from SIGTERM alone). Keep this single test's wait bounded.
   - `TestRun_DefaultModelResolved`: Manifest{DefaultModel:"glm-5-turbo", ModelFlag:"--model", Command:"/bin/echo", Subcommand captured}; pass `model==""`; assert the rendered args contain `--model glm-5-turbo` (default resolution happened). (Use a Command that echoes its args, or assert via a custom probe — simplest: Command="/bin/echo" and check `out` contains the flag+value.)
   - `TestRun_RenderErrorPropagates`: Manifest with an invalid PromptDelivery="telepathy" ⇒ Run returns the Render error, starts NO process.
   - `TestRun_EnvAdditionsMerged`: Manifest{Command:"/bin/sh", Subcommand:["-c","echo $SH_TEST"], Env:map[string]string{"SH_TEST":"xyz"}} ⇒ `out=="xyz\n"` (proves `cmd.Env=append(os.Environ(), r.Env...)`).
   - `TestRun_DirSetsCwd`: Manifest{Command:"/bin/pwd"}; Executor{Dir: t.TempDir()}; assert `out` is (trimmed) t.TempDir().
   - Use `tc:=tc` + `t.Run` only where a table is natural; the timeout/grace tests are individual functions (timing-sensitive). Each test builds its own `Manifest` literal + `Executor{}` (Dir="" ⇒ inherit stagehand's cwd, fine for echo/cat/sleep; the Dir test sets Dir explicitly).
8. Scope: ONLY `internal/provider/executor.go` + `internal/provider/executor_test.go`. Do NOT modify manifest.go/parse.go/registry.go/builtin.go or any of their tests, main.go, Makefile, go.mod, go.sum, internal/ui. No README/docs/providers TOML. No `go mod tidy` (stdlib-only). The `// Package provider` doc comment stays exactly once (in manifest.go).

### Known gotchas
```go
// CRITICAL: Setpgid makes the child its OWN group leader, so PGID == PID.
// Killing the GROUP requires the NEGATED pid: syscall.Kill(-cmd.Process.Pid, sig).
// A bare syscall.Kill(pid, sig) only fells the leader and LEAVES ORPHAN children.

// CRITICAL: use cmd.Start() + a goroutine running cmd.Wait(), NOT cmd.Run().
// cmd.Run blocks and cannot be raced against ctx.Done(); Start/Wait lets the
// select choose between natural exit and ctx cancel/timeout.

// CRITICAL: after ctx.Done() fires, you MUST drain the goroutine (read <-done)
// — otherwise the goroutine (and its cmd.Wait) leaks. The grace-select pattern
// (SIGTERM → <-done | 2s → SIGKILL → <-done) always reaches <-done.

// GOTCHA: SysProcAttr.Setpgid is unix-only (linux/darwin/freebsd). It compiles
// on the host (linux) so `go build ./internal/provider/` passes. A windows port
// (PRD §20.4 matrix) needs CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP/taskkill — DEFERRED, out of
// scope for this task. Do not gate this file behind a build tag now (the work
// item names a single executor.go and the host gate is linux).

// GOTCHA: FR25's timeout is applied by the CALLER (CommitStaged:
// context.WithTimeout(parent, cfg.Timeout)). Run takes ctx, has NO timeout
// parameter. Run distinguishes ctx.Err()==DeadlineExceeded (→TimeoutError) from
// Canceled (→cancel error). Tests wrap ctx with context.WithTimeout/Cancel.

// GOTCHA: when cmd.Stdin is nil, os/exec connects the child's stdin to
// /dev/null automatically — do NOT manually open os.DevNull. Only set
// cmd.Stdin = bytes.NewReader([]byte(r.StdinPayload)) when r.DeliverViaStdin.

// GOTCHA: the §19 "no sh -c" rule applies to the PRODUCT (Run execs m.Command
// with []string). Tests MAY use /bin/sh -c '...' as a fake m.Command body —
// that is a legitimate manifest choice, not a product violation. Run never
// interpolates args into a shell.

// GOTCHA: Render does NOT resolve DefaultModel/DefaultProvider (it is pure).
// Run must resolve them (if model=="" → m.DefaultModel; if provider=="" →
// m.DefaultProvider) BEFORE calling m.Render, per the P1.M2.T1.S1 seam.
```

## Implementation Blueprint

### Run pseudocode (verified on host — see research note §3)
```go
func (e *Executor) Run(ctx context.Context, m Manifest, model, provider, sys, payload string) (string, error) {
    // (a) resolve defaults (Render is pure and does not) — per P1.M2.T1.S1 seam
    if model == "" {
        model = m.DefaultModel
    }
    if provider == "" {
        provider = m.DefaultProvider
    }
    // (b) render the intent into []string args + stdin payload + env additions
    r, err := m.Render(model, provider, sys, payload)
    if err != nil {
        return "", err // e.g. unknown prompt_delivery
    }
    // (c) build the command — NO sh -c (§19); Args exclude the command token
    cmd := exec.Command(m.Command, r.Args...)
    cmd.Dir = e.Dir
    if r.DeliverViaStdin {
        cmd.Stdin = bytes.NewReader([]byte(r.StdinPayload))
    } // else nil ⇒ child gets /dev/null
    cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), r.Env...)
    cmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true} // §18.4
    var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer
    cmd.Stdout = &stdout
    cmd.Stderr = &stderr
    // (d) start + race Wait vs ctx
    if err := cmd.Start(); err != nil {
        return "", fmt.Errorf("provider %q: failed to start %q: %w", m.Name, m.Command, err)
    }
    pgid := cmd.Process.Pid // Setpgid ⇒ PID == PGID
    done := make(chan error, 1)
    go func() { done <- cmd.Wait() }()
    select {
    case err := <-done:
        if err == nil {
            return stdout.String(), nil // FR24: capture stdout
        }
        return "", &AgentError{ // non-zero exit / unexpected signal
            Name: m.Name, Command: m.Command,
            Code: exitCodeOf(err), Stderr: excerpt(stderr.String()),
        }
    case <-ctx.Done():
        cause := ctx.Err()
        // kill the whole GROUP (§18.4): SIGTERM, 2s grace, SIGKILL
        _ = syscall.Kill(-pgid, syscall.SIGTERM)
        select {
        case <-done:
        case <-time.After(2 * time.Second):
            _ = syscall.Kill(-pgid, syscall.SIGKILL)
            <-done // always drain
        }
        if cause == context.DeadlineExceeded {
            return "", &TimeoutError{Deadline: time.Now()}
        }
        return "", cause // *context.canceled
    }
}

func exitCodeOf(err error) int {
    var ee *exec.ExitError
    if errors.As(err, &ee) {
        return ee.ExitCode()
    }
    return -1
}

func excerpt(s string) string {
    s = strings.TrimSpace(s)
    if len(s) > 500 {
        s = s[len(s)-500:]
    }
    return s
}

// Typed errors:
type TimeoutError struct{ Deadline time.Time }
func (e *TimeoutError) Error() string { return "provider: generation timed out" }
func (e *TimeoutError) Unwrap() error { return context.DeadlineExceeded }

type AgentError struct{ Name, Command string; Code int; Stderr string }
func (e *AgentError) Error() string {
    return fmt.Sprintf("provider %q: %s exited %d: %s", e.Name, e.Command, e.Code, e.Stderr)
}
```

### Integration Points

CONSUMERS (downstream, DO NOT implement here):

  - internal/generate/signal.go (P1.M6.T2.S1): installSignalHandler(ctxCancel func(), rescueFn); on SIGINT/SIGTERM → ctxCancel() → ctx.Done() fires in Run → group killed → then rescue. restoreSignalHandler() BEFORE UpdateRefCAS.

NOT HERE: timeout config (caller's), the signal handler itself (M6.T2), rescue text (M6.T1.S3), prompt assembly (M4), git plumbing (M3), TOML/config (M5).

- `go test ./internal/provider/` → PASS (stdin-fed-exactly, stdout-captured, AgentError-on-nonzero, TimeoutError-on-timeout+group-kill, Canceled-on-cancel, grace-SIGKILL-on-ignored-TERM, default-model, Render-error, env-merge, Dir).
- `go test ./...` → whole-module integrity (Makefile `test` target green; manifest/parse/builtin/registry/ui tests still pass).

### Level 3 — (none beyond L2 here; the generate integration harness M6.T3 drives Run end-to-end against a stub provider. No service/HTTP/DB to integration-test.)

## DOCS Impact
**Mode A.** Per the work item: godoc on `Executor`, `Run`, `TimeoutError`, `AgentError` cites PRD §18.4 (Setpgid + process-group kill + grace SIGKILL), FR24 (capture stdout), FR25 (timeout→kill→rescue), and §19 (no sh -c). This inline godoc is the ONLY documentation this task produces. Do NOT create README.md/docs/providers TOML (PROVIDERS.md is fed in M8.T4.S2 from builtin.go). This PRP surfaces the item's declared DOCS line so it is not silently dropped.

## Anti-patterns to avoid
- ❌ Do NOT use `sh -c` in Run — exec `m.Command` directly with the `[]string` from Render (§19). (Tests MAY pick `/bin/sh -c '...'` as a fake m.Command body — that is a manifest choice, not a Run violation.)
- ❌ Do NOT kill only the leader PID (`syscall.Kill(pid,...)`) — you MUST negate it (`syscall.Kill(-pgid,...)`) to fell the whole process group, or agent children leak (§18.4).
- ❌ Do NOT use `cmd.Run()` (blocks; can't race ctx) — use `cmd.Start()` + a goroutine on `cmd.Wait()` + `select{done, ctx.Done()}`.
- ❌ Do NOT leak the Wait goroutine — always reach `<-done` (the grace-select guarantees it).
- ❌ Do NOT add a timeout PARAMETER to Run (the signature has none) — the caller applies FR25's timeout via `context.WithTimeout`; Run only inspects `ctx.Err()`.
- ❌ Do NOT add a stored context / public Cancel accessor / observe channel to Executor — the `ctx` PARAMETER is the M6.T2 signal-handler seam; over-building adds uncalled surface.
- ❌ Do NOT resolve DefaultModel/DefaultProvider inside Render (it is pure) — resolve them in Run BEFORE Render (per P1.M2.T1.S1 seam).
- ❌ Do NOT re-stringify Rendered.Args into a shell — exec `exec.Command(m.Command, r.Args...)` directly.
- ❌ Do NOT open os.DevNull manually for the no-stdin case — leaving `cmd.Stdin==nil` makes os/exec connect /dev/null automatically.
- ❌ Do NOT return bare sentinel errors — use typed `*TimeoutError` (Unwrap→DeadlineExceeded) + `*AgentError` so generate/rescue can `errors.As`/`Is` and extract Code/Stderr.
- ❌ Do NOT repeat the `// Package provider` doc comment (it stays in manifest.go); do NOT touch manifest.go/parse.go/registry.go/builtin.go/main.go/Makefile/go.mod/go.sum/internal/ui; do NOT run `go mod tidy` (stdlib-only).
implementationSteps: Create `internal/provider/executor.go` as a plain `package provider` file (manifest.go already OWNS the `// Package provider` doc — mirror how parse.go/registry.go/builtin.go use a plain `package provider` line; do NOT repeat the package doc). Import ONLY stdlib: `context`, `os/exec`, `syscall`, `bytes`, `time`, `fmt`, `strings`, `errors` (NO new module deps; this is why no go.mod/go.sum change is needed). Define two exported typed errors: `type TimeoutError struct { Deadline time.Time }` with `func (e *TimeoutError) Error() string { return "provider: generation timed out" }` and `func (e *TimeoutError) Unwrap() error { return context.DeadlineExceeded }` (so `errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded)` is true); and `type AgentError struct { Name string; Command string; Code int; Stderr string }` with an `Error()` that formats a clear one-liner (e.g. `fmt.Sprintf("provider %q: %s exited %d: %s", e.Name, e.Command, e.Code, e.Stderr)`). Define `type Executor struct { Dir string }` — ONLY the `Dir` field (repo root for cmd.Dir); do NOT add speculative fields (no logger/writer). Add `func NewExecutor(dir string) *Executor { return &Executor{Dir: dir} }`.

Implement `func (e *Executor) Run(ctx context.Context, m Manifest, model, provider, sys, payload string) (string, error)` in executor.go with full godoc citing PRD §18.4 (Setpgid + process-group kill + grace SIGKILL), FR24 (capture stdout), FR25 (timeout→kill→rescue), §19 (no sh -c), and noting the ctx-is-the-signal-handler-seam point. Body: (a) RESOLVE DEFAULTS before Render — `if model == "" { model = m.DefaultModel }`; `if provider == "" { provider = m.DefaultProvider }` (Render is pure and does NOT resolve defaults; this is the P1.M2.T1.S1 seam). (b) `r, err := m.Render(model, provider, sys, payload)`; on error return `"", err`. (c) Build `cmd := exec.Command(m.Command, r.Args...)` (Args EXCLUDE the command token; NO sh -c — §19); `cmd.Dir = e.Dir`; `if r.DeliverViaStdin { cmd.Stdin = bytes.NewReader([]byte(r.StdinPayload)) }` (else leave nil ⇒ os/exec connects the child's stdin to /dev/null automatically — do NOT open os.DevNull); `cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), r.Env...)`; `cmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}`; `var stdout, stderr bytes.Buffer; cmd.Stdout = &stdout; cmd.Stderr = &stderr`. (d) `if err := cmd.Start(); err != nil { return "", fmt.Errorf("provider %q: failed to start %q: %w", m.Name, m.Command, err) }`; capture `pgid := cmd.Process.Pid` (Setpgid ⇒ PID == PGID); `done := make(chan error, 1); go func() { done <- cmd.Wait() }()`. (e) `select { case err := <-done: if err == nil { return stdout.String(), nil }; return "", &AgentError{Name: m.Name, Command: m.Command, Code: exitCodeOf(err), Stderr: excerpt(stderr.String())} ; case <-ctx.Done(): cause := ctx.Err(); _ = syscall.Kill(-pgid, syscall.SIGTERM); select { case <-done: case <-time.After(2*time.Second): _ = syscall.Kill(-pgid, syscall.SIGKILL); <-done }; if cause == context.DeadlineExceeded { return "", &TimeoutError{Deadline: time.Now()} }; return "", cause }`. Add helpers `func exitCodeOf(err error) int` (errors.As into *exec.ExitError → ExitCode(); else -1) and `func excerpt(s string) string` (TrimSpace; last 500 chars if longer).

Create `internal/provider/executor_test.go` as `package provider` (white-box, stdlib `testing` ONLY — do NOT modify go.mod/go.sum). Each test builds a minimal inline `Manifest` literal + an `Executor{}` (Dir="" ⇒ inherit cwd, fine for echo/cat/sleep; the Dir test sets Dir explicitly). Tests (all using REAL host binaries as fake agents — NO real LLM, NO stub-binary generator): `TestRun_StdinFedExactly` (Manifest{Command:"/bin/cat", PromptDelivery:DeliveryStdin}, payload="FED-EXACT" ⇒ out=="FED-EXACT", err==nil); `TestRun_StdoutCaptured` (Manifest{Command:"/bin/echo", Subcommand:[]string{"hi"}, PromptDelivery:DeliveryPositional} ⇒ out=="hi\n", err==nil); `TestRun_AgentErrorNonZeroExit` (Manifest{Command:"/bin/sh", Subcommand:[]string{"-c","echo oops >&2; exit 7"}} ⇒ errors.As into *AgentError with .Code==7 and .Stderr containing "oops", out==""); `TestRun_TimeoutKillsGroup` (Manifest{Command:"/bin/sleep", Subcommand:[]string{"30"}}; ctx via context.WithTimeout(ctx, 80*time.Millisecond) ⇒ errors.As *TimeoutError, errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded), elapsed < ~1s proving SIGTERM killed it fast); `TestRun_CancelReturnsCanceled` (same sleep stub; context.WithCancel + cancel after 40ms in a goroutine ⇒ errors.Is(err, context.Canceled)); `TestRun_GraceSIGKILLOnIgnoredTERM` (Manifest{Command:"/bin/sh", Subcommand:[]string{"-c","trap '' TERM; sleep 30"}} (child ignores SIGTERM); context.WithTimeout(ctx,80ms) ⇒ *TimeoutError returned AND elapsed ≥ ~2s < ~3s, proving the 2s-grace SIGKILL escalation fired); `TestRun_DefaultModelResolved` (Manifest{DefaultModel:"glm-5-turbo", ModelFlag:"--model", Command:"/bin/echo", PromptDelivery:DeliveryPositional}; model=="" ⇒ out contains "--model glm-5-turbo"); `TestRun_RenderErrorPropagates` (Manifest with PromptDelivery="telepathy" ⇒ Run returns the Render error, no process started); `TestRun_EnvAdditionsMerged` (Manifest{Command:"/bin/sh", Subcommand:[]string{"-c","echo $SH_TEST"}, Env:map[string]string{"SH_TEST":"xyz"}} ⇒ out=="xyz\n"); `TestRun_DirSetsCwd` (Manifest{Command:"/bin/pwd"}; Executor{Dir: t.TempDir()} ⇒ strings.TrimSpace(out)==t.TempDir()). Use `t.Helper`/`t.Errorf`; `tc:=tc` + `t.Run` only where a table is natural (the timing-sensitive timeout/cancel/grace tests are individual funcs).

Run the validation gates IN ORDER and fix any failure before declaring done: `go build ./internal/provider/` (must compile into the existing package), `go vet ./internal/provider/` (clean), `test -z "$(gofmt -l internal/provider/)"` (no unformatted files — gofmt -l always exits 0 so wrap in test -z, the proven sibling idiom), `go test ./internal/provider/` (PASS — all executor tests + existing manifest/parse/builtin/registry tests still green), `go test ./...` (whole-module integrity; internal/ui still passes).

DOCS impact: Mode A — confirm `Executor`, `Run`, `TimeoutError`, and `AgentError` each have a godoc comment citing the relevant PRD anchors (§18.4 for Setpgid/process-group-kill/grace-SIGKILL; FR24 for stdout capture; FR25 for timeout→kill→rescue; §19 for no sh -c). Create NO README.md/docs/providers TOML. Confirm scope boundaries: ONLY `internal/provider/executor.go` and `internal/provider/executor_test.go` were created; manifest.go/manifest_test.go/parse.go/parse_test.go/builtin.go/builtin_test.go/registry.go/registry_test.go, cmd/stagehand/main.go, Makefile, go.mod, go.sum, and internal/ui are unchanged; no non-stdlib imports were added; no `go mod tidy` was run; the `// Package provider` doc comment still appears exactly once (in manifest.go).

successCriteria.description: internal/provider/executor.go exists as a plain `package provider` file (NOT repeating the package doc, which stays in manifest.go) and imports ONLY stdlib (context, os/exec, syscall, bytes, time, fmt, strings, errors) — no os/environ-only-no, no cobra/pflag/go-toml, no new module deps.

Executor struct has only a Dir string field; NewExecutor(dir string) *Executor constructor exists; Run(ctx context.Context, m Manifest, model, provider, sys, payload string) (string, error) exists with godoc citing PRD §18.4/FR24/FR25/§19.

Run resolves model/provider defaults (if model=="" → m.DefaultModel; if provider=="" → m.DefaultProvider) BEFORE calling m.Render, then builds exec.Command(m.Command, r.Args...) with NO sh -c (§19); sets cmd.Dir=e.Dir, cmd.Stdin from bytes.NewReader(r.StdinPayload) only when r.DeliverViaStdin (else nil), cmd.Env=append(os.Environ(), r.Env...), and cmd.SysProcAttr=&syscall.SysProcAttr{Setpgid:true}.

Run uses cmd.Start() + a goroutine running cmd.Wait() + select on the done channel vs ctx.Done(); on a clean exit it returns the captured stdout (FR24); on a non-zero exit it returns a typed *AgentError{Name,Command,Code,Stderr}.

On ctx.Done(), Run kills the whole process GROUP via syscall.Kill(-pgid, syscall.SIGTERM) then (if not exited within ~2s) syscall.Kill(-pgid, syscall.SIGKILL), drains the goroutine, and returns *TimeoutError when ctx.Err()==context.DeadlineExceeded (else ctx.Err() == context.Canceled).

TimeoutError is an exported type whose Unwrap() returns context.DeadlineExceeded (so errors.Is(err, context.DeadlineExceeded) is true); AgentError is an exported type carrying Name, Command, Code, and a short (~500 char) Stderr excerpt.

executor_test.go (package provider, stdlib testing) proves: stdin is fed byte-exactly (/bin/cat echoes verbatim), stdout is captured (/bin/echo), a non-zero-exit fake returns *AgentError with the stderr excerpt and Code, a hanging stub under context.WithTimeout is process-group-killed and returns *TimeoutError (~80ms, proving SIGTERM killed it fast), an explicitly-cancelled ctx returns context.Canceled, and a SIGTERM-ignoring child (/bin/sh -c 'trap "" TERM; sleep 30') under a timeout returns *TimeoutError with elapsed ≥ ~2s (proving the grace SIGKILL escalation).

executor_test.go also covers default-model resolution (model=="" → m.DefaultModel appears in the rendered args), Render-error propagation (invalid PromptDelivery returns the Render error with no process started), env-additions merge (manifest Env appears in the child's environment), and Dir wiring (Executor.Dir sets the child cwd).
go test ./internal/provider/ exits 0 (PASS) and go test ./... exits 0 (whole-module integrity; existing manifest/parse/builtin/registry/ui tests still pass).
go build ./internal/provider/ and go vet ./internal/provider/ are clean, and gofmt -l internal/provider/ produces no output.

Scope boundaries respected: only internal/provider/executor.go and internal/provider/executor_test.go created; manifest.go, parse.go, registry.go, builtin.go and their tests, cmd/stagehand/main.go, Makefile, go.mod, go.sum, and internal/ui are unchanged; no README/docs/providers TOML created; no go mod tidy; the // Package provider doc comment appears exactly once (in manifest.go).

references: PRD.md §12.2 (lines 493-518) — the exec construction Run consumes: cmd = exec.Command(m.command, args...); cmd.Stdin = bytes.NewReader (delivery stdin) else /dev/null; cmd.Env = os.Environ()+m.env. file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md
PRD.md FR24 (line 268) + FR25 (line 269) — capture stdout; configurable generation timeout (default 120s) → kill the agent → rescue path. file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md

PRD.md §18.4 (lines 1192-1199) — SysProcAttr.Setpgid=true so the whole process tree is killable; SIGTERM then SIGKILL after a grace period via the process group. file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md

PRD.md §19 (lines 1201-1212) — security: commands built as []string and exec'd directly, NEVER sh -c; diff delivered via stdin never interpolated into an argument. file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/PRD.md

plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/decisions.md §5 — restates render→exec, Env=os.Environ()+m.Env, SysProcAttr.Setpgid=true for process-group kill on signal; confirms Rendered.Args exclude the command and Rendered.Env are additions only.
plan/001_f1f80943ac34/architecture/reference_impl.md §7 — process-group signal handling with grace-period SIGKILL is the genuinely-new engineering surface (NOT in commit-pi, which just trap'd).

internal/provider/manifest.go (P1.M2.T1.S1, COMPLETE) — the dependency: Manifest.Render(model,provider,sys,user)(*Rendered,error) is PURE; Rendered{Args []string, StdinPayload string, DeliverViaStdin bool, Env []string}; Render does NOT resolve DefaultModel/DefaultProvider (Run must, before Render). file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/internal/provider/manifest.go

internal/provider/registry.go — the package's existing os/exec idiom (exec.LookPath) and plain `package provider` line + doc density to mirror. file:///home/dustin/projects/stagehand-hack/internal/provider/registry.go

plan/001_f1f80943ac34/tasks.json P1.M6.T2.S1 (signal handler: installSignalHandler(ctxCancel,rescueFn) → on signal calls ctxCancel, which cancels the ctx Run selects on) + P1.M6.T1.S1 (generate owns ctx with WithTimeout(cfg.Timeout), threads ctx to Run and cancel to the signal handler) — confirms the ctx parameter is the seam; no stored context/Cancel accessor is needed on Executor.

plan/001_f1f80943ac34/prps/research/P1_M2_T4_S1_research.md — verified-on-host (go1.26.4 linux/amd64) mechanics: Setpgid⇒PID==PGID; syscall.Kill(-pgid,SIGTERM) fells the group; Start+goroutine-Wait+select(done,ctx.Done); 2s-grace SIGKILL; typed TimeoutError/AgentError; mocking with /bin/echo|cat|sh|sleep; full prototype PASSED.
os/exec.Cmd — Start/Wait/Stdin/Stdout/Stderr/Env/SysProcAttr/Process.Pid; nil Stdin ⇒ child gets /dev/null automatically. https://pkg.go.dev/os/exec#Cmd
syscall.SysProcAttr.Setpgid (unix-only: linux/darwin/freebsd) — makes the child a process-group leader (PGID==PID). https://pkg.go.dev/syscall#SysProcAttr
syscall.Kill — Kill(-pgid, sig) sends the signal to every process in the group (negative pid ⇒ group, not the single leader). https://pkg.go.dev/syscall#Kill
context.Context — ctx.Done()/Err(); DeadlineExceeded vs Canceled distinction drives TimeoutError vs cancel error. https://pkg.go.dev/context#Context
errors.As / errors.Is — typed *TimeoutError (Unwrap→DeadlineExceeded) + *AgentError so generate/rescue can route on them. https://pkg.go.dev/errors
